title: Saving and Loading XML Streams

Saving and Loading XML Streams

Saving and Loading XML Streams

You can exchange data between components using XML streams, and reconnect disconnected record sets held in XML streams. The structure of an XML stream is defined by the rules specified in a DTD. The XML stream parameter type allows you to send disconnected record sets between components.

To ensure that components can reconnect disconnected record sets, the id, crc, and status attributes must be defined in the DTD for the XML stream. See Working with DTDs.

  1. To create an XML stream:
    1. Place the data destined for the XML stream into component fields of the type database or non-database. This can involve retrieving the data from a database, or creating and modifying field values by ProcScript.
    2. Define a variable or parameter with data type xmlstream to contain the XML stream.
    3. Save the data into the XML stream variable or parameter using the xmlsave statement.
      This statement uses switches and arguments to manipulate the construction of the XML stream, and creates the structure of the XML stream according to the rules specified in the DTD.
    4. Send the XML stream as an OUT parameter, or save it to file for further use.
  2. To read an XML stream:
    1. Define an operation with an XML stream as an IN parameter and declare the type to be xmlstream.
    2. Load the data from the XML stream into the component's data structure using the xmlload ProcScript statement.

      This statement uses switches and arguments to control the transfer of data from the XML stream to the component.

      After the xmlload statement has executed, the data in the XML stream is available as disconnected occurrences in the component.

  3. To reconnect data from an XML stream to component and database data:
    1. Load the data from the XML stream using xmlload.
    2. Use retrieve/reconnect to merge the data from the XML stream with the existing occurrences in the component and the database (if connected).
      After the retrieve/reconnect statement has executed, the data in the XML stream is available as normal Uniface occurrences in the component.
    3. On database entities, use store to update the database with the loaded data.
Related Links
  • XML Streams

    XML streams are data streams using an XML format. They enable loosely coupled connections between components, and provide a standardized format for data transport between components. Uniface can transfer field data and data relationships via XML stream parameters. It can also attach processing information to data in an XML stream to allow the data state to be transported with the data.
